Moonseed (Menispermum canadense) is a perennial vine native to eastern North America, characterized by its heart-shaped leaves and small, blue-black berries. The plant is used in herbal medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which have been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Moonseed include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Moonseed include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation, reduce pain, and support immune function.
